segments.text: "Sometime later, during the third year, a message from the Lord came to Elijah:
Go and appear before Ahab, and I will send rain on the earth.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: "So Elijah went to appear before Ahab. In the meantime the famine had become severe in Samaria.
"Ahab summoned Obadiah, the manager of his palace (Obadiah was a very sincere believer in the Lord.
"While Jezebel was busy killing the Lord's prophets, Obadiah had taken one hundred prophets and hidden them, fifty in each of two caves, and had provided them with food and water.)
"Ahab told Obadiah,
Go throughout the country and check all the springs and valleys. Perhaps we can find some grass to keep the horses and mules alive so we will not lose any of the animals.
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: "So they divided the land between them. Ahab went in one direction, and Obadiah the other.
"As Obadiah went on his way, Elijah came to meet him. Obadiah recognized him, bowed down to the ground, and said,
Is it you, my lord Elijah?
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: It's me,
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: Elijah replied.
Go and tell your master, ‘Elijah is here.’
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: How have I sinned that you are handing me, your servant, over to Ahab to be killed?
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: As the Lord your God lives, there's no nation or kingdom where my master hasn't sent someone to search for you. When a nation or kingdom said you weren't there, he made them swear that they couldn't find you.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: And now you're telling me to go to my master and announce, ‘Elijah is here!’
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: I have no idea where the Spirit of the Lord will take you after I leave you. If I go and tell Ahab and then he can't find you, he's going to kill me, even though I, your servant, have worshiped the Lord since I was young.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: Didn't you hear, my lord, what I did when Jezebel was busy killing the Lord's prophets? I hid a hundred of the Lord's prophets, fifty in each of two caves, and provided them with food and water.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: And now you're telling me to go to my master and announce, ‘Elijah is here.’ He's going to kill me!
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: "Elijah replied,
As the Lord Almighty lives, the one I serve, I will definitely appear before Ahab today.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: "So Obadiah went to meet Ahab and told him, and then Ahab went to meet Elijah.
"When Ahab saw Elijah, he said to him,
Is that you—you who are causing trouble for Israel?
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: I'm not causing trouble for Israel,
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: Elijah replied.
It's you and your father's family! You have rejected the Lord's commands and are worshiping the Baals.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: Now call together all of Israel and meet me on Mount Carmel, along with the four hundred and fifty prophets of Baal and the four hundred prophets of Asherah, who are supported by Jezebel.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: "So Ahab summoned all of Israel and also gathered the prophets on Mount Carmel.
"Elijah approached the people and asked them,
How long will you hobble along, hesitating between two opposite beliefs? If the Lord is God, then follow him. But if Baal is God, then follow him.
segments.speaker: Elijah approached the people and

segments.text: But the people didn't answer.
"Then Elijah told them,
I am the only one left of the Lord's prophets—just me alone—but Baal has four hundred and fifty prophets.
segments.speaker: Elijah approached the people and

segments.text: Provide us with two bulls. Let the prophets of Baal choose for themselves the one they want, and have them cut it into pieces and place it on the firewood. But don't set fire to it. I will prepare the other bull and place it on the firewood but I won't set fire to it.
segments.speaker: Elijah approached the people and

segments.text: Then you call on your god by name, and I will call on the Lord by name. The god who replies by sending fire—he is God.
segments.speaker: all the people

segments.text: Then all the people said,
We agree to what you say.
segments.speaker: all the people

segments.text: "Elijah said to the prophets of Baal,
You choose one of the bulls and prepare it first, because there are so many of you. Call on your god by name, but don't light the fire.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: "So they took the bull provided and prepared it. Then they called on Baal by name from morning until noon.
Baal, answer us!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they pleaded. But no voice was heard, no one answered. They hobbled[^51] in a dance around the altar they had made.
"At noon Elijah began to mock them.
Shout really loud!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he said.
Isn't he meant to be a god? Maybe he's meditating, or he's gone to the bathroom, or he's away on a trip! Maybe he's asleep and has to be woken up!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: "They shouted even louder and cut themselves with swords and spears until they bled. This was their usual way of worshiping.
"Noon came and went, and they went on with their manic
prophesying
segments.speaker: no voice was heard, no one

segments.text: until the time[^52] of the evening sacrifice. But no voice was heard, no one answered, no one was listening.
"Then Elijah told everyone,
Come over here to me.
segments.speaker: no voice was heard, no one

segments.text: They went over to him, and he repaired the altar of the Lord that had been torn down.
"Elijah took twelve stones to represent the tribes of the sons of Jacob. (Jacob was the one who received the Lord's message that said,
Israel shall be your name.
segments.speaker: no voice was heard, no one

segments.text: "With the stones he built an altar in the Lord's name. He dug a ditch around it that could hold two seahs of seed.
"He put the wood in place, cut the bull into pieces, and laid it on the wood. Then he told them,
Fill up four large jars with water and pour it over the offering and the wood.
segments.speaker: no voice was heard, no one

segments.text: Do it again,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he said. So they did.
Do it for a third time,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he said. So they did it for the third time.
"The water ran down all over the altar and even filled the ditch.
"At the time of the evening sacrifice, the prophet Elijah went over to the altar and prayed:
Lord, God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, demonstrate today that you are God in Israel, that I am your servant, and that everything I have done has been at your command.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: Answer me, Lord! Answer me, so that these people will know that you, Lord, are God, and that you are bringing them back to you.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: "Then the fire of the Lord came down and burned up the sacrifice, the wood, the stones, and the soil—it even licked up the water in the ditch!
"When all the people saw this, they fell facedown on the ground and shouted,
The Lord, he is God! The Lord, he is God!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: "Then Elijah ordered them,
Grab hold of the prophets of Baal. Don't let any escape!
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: They grabbed them, and Elijah brought them down to the Kishon Valley and killed them there.
"Elijah told Ahab,
Go and eat and drink, for I hear the sound of a heavy rain coming.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: "So Ahab went to eat and drink, but Elijah went to the top of Carmel. There he bent down to the ground, putting his face between his knees.
Go and look toward the sea,
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: he told his servant. The man went and looked.
There's nothing there,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he said. Seven times Elijah told him,
Go and look again.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: "The seventh time the servant came back and said,
I saw a small cloud the size of a man's hand coming up from the sea.
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: So Elijah said,
Run to Ahab and tell him, ‘Get your chariot ready and go down before the rain stops you.’
segments.speaker: Elijah

segments.text: "Very quickly the sky grew dark with clouds, the wind blew, heavy rain started falling, and Ahab rode down to Jezreel.
"The Lord gave his power to Elijah—he tucked his cloak into his belt and ran ahead of Ahab all the way to Jezreel.
